pattern: Row order and column order are two independent topological sorts over the numbers, and their combined positions place each value into a unique cell of the grid.
hints: The row constraints and column constraints are separate ordering problems; solve each as its own dependency ordering.
A topological sort of the row constraints gives each number a row index, and the same on the column constraints gives a column index.
If either ordering has a cycle, no matrix exists, so return an empty list; otherwise place value v at the cell whose row is its rank in the row order and whose column is its rank in the column order.

edge_inputs.description: row constraints form a cycle, no matrix
minimum size with one constraint each

stress.description: small k with a random DAG of row and column constraints (acyclic by construction)
stress.note: constraints drawn from a fixed permutation are guaranteed acyclic, so a matrix always exists
